featured.title: Berlin U-Bahn Fine: What Happens If You Are Caught Without a Ticket
featured.label: Ticket check help
featured.summary: A practical guide for the moment a Berlin ticket check goes wrong: the EUR 60 EBE fine, unstamped paper tickets, wrong zones, forgotten passes and official next steps.
featured.alt: Yellow U-Bahn train at Prinzenstrasse station in Berlin

miniPosts.title: Berlin Before Hotel Check-In: What to Do With Luggage and Time
miniPosts.summary: A first-day guide for the awkward hours before your room is ready: luggage first, one small area, and a simple way to use the time without dragging bags around Berlin.
miniPosts.alt: Rolling suitcases at Berlin Hauptbahnhof, showing the luggage problem before hotel check-in in Berlin.

miniPosts.title: Berlin Transport Strike: How to Keep Moving When Trains Stop
miniPosts.summary: A practical route and transport fallback guide for blocked lines, with what to check and when to switch modes.

miniPosts.title: Berliner Dom Tickets: Dome Climb, Crypt and Visit Tips
miniPosts.summary: Decide whether Berliner Dom tickets are worth it for your day, with the dome climb, crypt and Museum Island context in one practical read.
miniPosts.alt: Berliner Dom and Alte Nationalgalerie on Museum Island at sunset

miniPosts.title: Berlin Train Stations: Hauptbahnhof, Ostbahnhof, Südkreuz and Arrival Tips
miniPosts.summary: A practical arrival guide to Berlin's main train stations, including which station to use and what to do first after stepping off the train.
miniPosts.alt: S-Bahn train arriving inside Berlin Hauptbahnhof, the main railway station for many tourists arriving by train.

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline

posts.title: Berlin Public Transport for Tourists: Tickets, Zones, Prices and Validation
posts.summary: Berlin public transport uses one shared ticket system for U-Bahn, S-Bahn, tram and bus. This guide covers zones, prices, validation, BER airport trips and the EUR 60 fine.
posts.alt: Yellow train speeding through Konstanzer Strasse station. The wall has bold stripes and text. Ceiling lights illuminate the platform.

posts.title: Berlin Public Holidays 2026: What Tourists Need to Know
posts.summary: A practical tourist guide to Berlin public holidays in 2026, including exact dates, shop closures, museum hours, transport, food backups and what to do instead.
posts.alt: Berlin skyline with cathedral and TV tower, German flag waving, a river boat below, and pink blossoms in the foreground
